landing.subhead: TradingAgents runs a full desk over any ticker — specialist analysts, a bull-vs-bear research debate, a trader, and a risk committee — then streams every step of their reasoning to your browser in real time.
landing.stagesSub: Each ticker flows through specialized roles. The handoffs are exactly what you watch stream live on the run page.

stage.analystsBody: Market, sentiment, news, and fundamentals analysts each file a grounded report.
stage.researchBody: Bull and bear researchers argue it out over multiple rounds; a manager synthesizes.
stage.traderBody: The trader turns the research into a concrete investment plan.
stage.riskBody: Aggressive, conservative, and neutral analysts stress-test the plan.
stage.pmBody: The PM issues the final BUY / SELL / HOLD with a rated confidence.

landing.featuresTitle: Built for watching the work, not just the verdict
landing.featuresSub: The CLI gave you a final call. The workbench gives you the whole desk — live, replayable, and comparable.

feature.streamingBody: Watch every report, tool call, and debate message arrive over SSE — pause, resume, and replay.
feature.debateBody: Debates render as side-by-side bubbles by round, so you can read the argument, not just the result.
feature.logBody: Every run is recorded with its realized return and an after-the-fact reflection on what worked.
feature.chartsBody: Candlesticks with your historical decisions overlaid, plus the indicators the analysts cite.
feature.modelsBody: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Qwen, GLM, and more — across every market Yahoo Finance covers.
feature.compareBody: Re-run the same ticker on different models and put the decisions side by side.

landing.ctaBody: Pick a ticker and a date, choose your models, and watch the agents debate it out — from the first analyst report to the portfolio manager's final call.

dashboard.title: Workbench
dashboard.subtitle: Your analyses, decisions, and outcomes at a glance.

step4.outputLanguage: Output language (what the agents write in)
